data.narrative:
Ocius Technology is a Series B defense and security company founded by Robert Dane. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of six same-sector, same-stage private companies, supplemented by two public defense-tech references (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given available data. The six Defense & Security Series B comparables display valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B, with a median of $1.2B. Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI anchor the upper quartile at $1.4B–$1.5B; Castelion and Defense Unicorns establish the floor at $1.0B. This distribution supports a central valuation estimate of $1.2B.

The triangulated range spans $1.0B (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $1.2B. Key uncertainties include the revenue-generating capacity and stage of maturity relative to peers, as well as the strategic value of the A$176M Australian Program of Record contract secured in March 2026. Given the single-method foundation and reliance on private peer comps, corroboration through revenue multiples or enterprise value–to-contract backlog analysis would strengthen conviction. The analysis supports a valuation centered near $1.2B, with defensible range bounds of $1.0B–$1.4B absent material new information on commercialization trajectory or customer concentration.
